statement.normalized: Let $B_2(n)$ be the $2$-full part of $n$ (that is, $B_2(n)=n/n'$ where $n'$ is the product of all primes that divide $n$ exactly once). Is it true that, for every fixed $k\geq 1$,\[\prod_{n\leq m<n+k}B_2(m) \ll n^{2+o(1)}?\]Or perhaps even $\ll_k n^2$?
statement.original: Let $B_2(n)$ be the $2$-full part of $n$ (that is, $B_2(n)=n/n'$ where $n'$ is the product of all primes that divide $n$ exactly once). Is it true that, for every fixed $k\geq 1$,\[\prod_{n\leq m<n+k}B_2(m) \ll n^{2+o(1)}?\]Or perhaps even $\ll_k n^2$?
